score_notes.visual: The dark aesthetic is genuinely beautiful. Vibe-reactive borders, the ficus, PANIC screens, custom character SVGs — all lovely. Knocked down for the bleed: the office sunrise window is visibly poking outside the container border in the desktop screenshot like it's trying to escape. overflow:visible is doing exactly what it says, unfortunately.
score_notes.ux: Keyboard shortcuts, tab navigation, ping reactions, activity filters, solo spotlight — this is a thoughtfully designed UX. But: Bloberto's speech bubble overlaps the tab toggle on mobile (see screenshot), making it temporarily impossible to switch views. That's not a vibe — that's a trap.
score_notes.responsiveness: Big win: the mobile office is NOW VISIBLE. Dashboard review #58's most embarrassing issue is resolved. However: on mobile the office still feels cramped, the speech bubble covers the tab navigation bar, and elements near the right edge of the office-floor still bleed outside the container. Progress, not perfection.
score_notes.performance: Good choices throughout — React.memo, useMemo, useCallback, polling intervals, AbortController on fetch. The triple visibilitychange listeners are inefficient but not catastrophic. No perf regressions found.
score_notes.accessibility: Carry-over strikes 16 on two critical issues: aria-live still lives on conditional elements (screen readers get ghost announcements when elements unmount), and overflow:visible still causes boundary bleed which affects focus containment. The pingReaction sr-only span is a nice addition but doesn't offset the architectural sins.
score_notes.code_quality: Carlos shipped five fixes cleanly — titleIdxRef, footer counter, dead updatedAt prop, after-hours greeting, TASK_TAG_DEFS pattern — all confirmed fixed. Maya fixed the toast overlap with a vertical offset. However: the inline toast styles remain with ~16 props each in JSX, duplicate CSS attribute-selector rules litter Office.css (single-quoted AND double-quoted versions of the same .office-floor[data-vibe] rules), and three simultaneous visibilitychange listeners remain. The tech debt is accumulating interest.
score_notes.features: Conference table standup detection, ficus growth streak, ghost workers, lone-survivor labels, vibe sparkline, ping reactions, activity log with grouped time sections, clickable stat cards that scroll to workers — the feature density is impressive and most of it works. The feature velocity is admirable even if the cleanup velocity is not.
score_notes.fun_factor: PANIC screens on error, the office clock with vibe-specific commentary ('Time has no meaning during an incident'), Bloberto's crown that updates based on vibe, away sticky notes, the ficus that mourns when the streak dies. This thing is a delight. The only thing preventing a 5 is the speech bubble that covers the UI — even comedy has to not break the interface.

carry_over_status.evidence: Office.css line 84: `.office-floor { overflow: visible }` unchanged. Visually confirmed in desktop screenshot: the sunrise window element bleeds outside the container border at the right edge. Characters near edges also escape. Fix is one line: `overflow: hidden` (or `clip` for better subpixel handling).
carry_over_status.note: STRIKE 16. This has now been ignored through sixteen consecutive reviews. The office has no walls.

carry_over_status.issue: aria-live on conditional elements — mounts/unmounts cause phantom announcements
carry_over_status.evidence: Still present in 5 locations: App.jsx:1141 (completionToast), Office.jsx:1283 (plantToast), Office.jsx:1316 (isFullSync banner), Office.jsx:1317 (isFullIdle banner), Office.jsx:1475 (vibeCaption). All are conditional renders with aria-live directly on the element. Screen readers announce the removal. Fix: use always-mounted containers with aria-live, change content inside them.
carry_over_status.note: STRIKE 16. Every screen reader user is getting phantom announcements on every vibe change. This is not a feature.

carry_over_status.evidence: Mobile screenshot confirms the office view renders correctly at 375x812. Media queries at max-width:480px set min-height:65vh and height:calc(100vw*0.75). Workers are visible and positioned. Issue resolved.
carry_over_status.note: ✅ Fixed. Welcome to the mobile era, Bloberto.

carry_over_status.evidence: App.jsx lines 852, 874, and 1093 each call document.addEventListener('visibilitychange'). Three separate effects register three concurrent listeners: handleVisibilityResume (resume polling), handleVisibilityChange (adjust poll interval), and the tab-title handleVisibilityChange (adjust title). Each cleanup is correct individually, but three listeners firing sequentially on every tab switch is wasteful and creates ordering dependencies.
carry_over_status.note: Consolidate into one visibilitychange effect that handles all three concerns.

carry_over_status.issue: Inline toast styles — ~16 props each hardcoded in JSX
carry_over_status.evidence: completionToast (App.jsx:1143-1162) has 16 inline style props. plantToast (Office.jsx:1287-1303) has 15 inline style props. Maya fixed the OVERLAP (plantToast now at bottom:5.5rem, completionToast at bottom:2rem — good), but neither has been extracted to CSS classes. Both toasts share nearly identical styling. A `.toast-popup` base class would eliminate 28 of those 31 shared props.
carry_over_status.note: Maya half-shipped this. The overlap is fixed. The inline style smell remains.

carlos_fixes_verified.fix: titleIdxRef — tab title rotation index persists across re-renders
carlos_fixes_verified.evidence: App.jsx line 705: `const titleIdxRef = useRef(0)`. Used in title effect at lines 1065-1069 to preserve index. Previously a local `let idx` would reset to 0 on every effect re-run.
Footer renders `{Math.min(quoteBonus, BONUS_TAGLINES.length)}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length}` counter correctly. quoteBonus increments on click, counter caps at 8/8.
formatDuration() is correctly called with worker.updated_at throughout. WorkerCard at line 345-348 uses worker.updated_at for duration tier calculation.
App.jsx line 1103: `const isAfterHours = currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 20`. Correctly treats before-5am and after-8pm as after-hours. Greeting shows '🌃 Burning the midnight oil' in those windows.
getTaskTags() at App.jsx:181 uses `.filter(({ pattern }) => pattern.test(taskString)).map(({ label, emoji, className }) => ({ label, emoji, className }))` — clean filter+map pattern, no mutation.

maya_fixes_verified.evidence: plantToast now positioned at bottom:'5.5rem' (Office.jsx:1288), completionToast at bottom:'2rem' (App.jsx:1144). They no longer collide. Inline styles still present (see carry-over), but the overlap itself is resolved.

suggestions.title: ESCALATED: aria-live on conditional elements — strike 16
suggestions.description:
Five locations still mount/unmount elements with aria-live directly on them. Screen readers announce element removal as phantom content. Fix: create always-mounted wrapper containers with aria-live='polite', then swap their text content. Example: replace `{completionToast &&
…
}` with `
{completionToast ? '...' : ''}
`. Apply to: completionToast, plantToast, isFullSync banner, isFullIdle banner, vibeCaption. This is 16 reviews of phantom screen reader announcements.

suggestions.title: ESCALATED: overflow:visible on .office-floor — strike 16
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: `.office-floor { overflow: visible }` allows children to escape the container boundary. Visually confirmed in desktop screenshot — the sunrise window element bleeds past the right border. Fix: change to `overflow: hidden` or `overflow: clip`. If progress rings or speech bubbles need to extend outside desks internally, use `overflow: clip` to allow paint-layer overflow without scrollbar creation. Characters near the edge currently escape into the page margin.

suggestions.title: Speech bubble covers tab navigation on mobile
suggestions.description: On mobile (375px), Bloberto's speech bubble renders at the top of the office-floor and visually overlaps the tab toggle bar (confirmed in mobile screenshot). The tab bar becomes temporarily unpressable when a speech bubble is active. Fix: add `pointer-events: none` to speech bubbles (they're informational only), and ensure z-index layering doesn't stack bubbles above the tab nav. Alternatively, the office-floor's top positioning should account for the header + tab bar height on mobile.

suggestions.title: Duplicate CSS attribute selectors for .office-floor[data-vibe]
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 define .office-floor[data-vibe='*'] with single-quoted attributes (border-color + box-shadow). Lines 97-115 then redefine .office-floor[data-vibe="*"] with double-quoted attributes adding --grid-line AND re-declaring border-color + box-shadow. This is ~10 duplicate declarations. The single-quoted block at lines 91-95 should be removed entirely — the double-quoted block that follows supersedes it. CSS selector matching ignores quote style so both match, causing double cascade weight.

suggestions.title: Consolidate triple visibilitychange listeners — strike 4
suggestions.description: Three separate useEffect hooks each register a visibilitychange listener (App.jsx:852, 874, 1093). While each effect correctly removes its own listener on cleanup, three concurrent listeners fire sequentially on every tab switch. Consolidate into a single effect that: (1) resumes polling on tab-visible, (2) adjusts poll interval, (3) updates tab title. This reduces listener registration overhead and eliminates the ordering dependency between the three effects.

suggestions.title: Extract toast popup styles to CSS class — strike 4
suggestions.description: completionToast (App.jsx:1143) has 16 inline style props; plantToast (Office.jsx:1287) has 15. They share 13 identical declarations. Extract to a `.toast-popup` CSS class containing the fixed styles (position:fixed, left:50%, transform:translateX(-50%), border-radius:12px, padding, font-size, font-weight, color, z-index:9999, white-space:nowrap, pointer-events:none, animation). Keep dynamic styles (border-color, box-shadow for color) as inline. Residual inline props: 2-3 each. Maya fixed the overlap but left the style sprawl.

suggestions.title: Conference table 'VACANT' label renders at 0.38rem — unreadable
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx around line 1015, the lastHuddle elapsed-time display uses `fontSize: '0.38rem'`. At 375px mobile width, 0.38rem ≈ 6px. That is not text, that is a rumor of text. Either remove this micro-annotation or size it to at least 0.65rem. The desktop screenshot shows the conference table area with this ghost text that serves no practical purpose at its current size.

suggestions.title: Task tag spans lack keyboard navigation roles when interactive
suggestions.description: When onTagClick is provided, task tags in WorkerCard (App.jsx:325-338) get role='button' and tabIndex=0 with onKeyDown. But the role='button' requires aria-pressed to communicate toggle state. The isActive state (selectedTag === label) should be reflected as aria-pressed={isActive} so screen readers know the filter is currently applied. Without it, keyboard users activating a filter get no feedback that the tag is now 'pressed'.

suggestions.title: Lone survivor label at 0.45rem font-size is unreadable everywhere
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1538: `fontSize: '0.45rem'` on the lone-survivor label. On standard screens, 0.45rem ≈ 7.2px — below the minimum legible threshold of 11px. The charming worker-specific captions ('📊 no bottlenecks when you ARE the stack') deserve to actually be read. Use at minimum 0.65rem, or show the message in the vibe weather label instead.

suggestions.title: Sparkline legend only appears on hover — inaccessible and unreliable
suggestions.description: App.css: `.sparkline-legend { display: none }` shown only on `.stat-card:hover`. On mobile and keyboard-only navigation, this legend is permanently hidden. The vibe history sparkline dots are color-coded but the legend that explains them is hover-gated. At minimum show it on :focus-within as well. Better: always show the last 3 pills below the sparkline (they're tiny, the space is there).

suggestions.title: workers.json out of sync with deployed data structure
suggestions.description: The local /workspace/workers.json only contains Luna with progress:0 and no roster array. The live site fetches from GitHub and has all 5 workers in 'Crushing It' vibe. This makes local development/testing misleading — anyone running locally sees a completely different experience than production. Add a realistic multi-worker sample that exercises the roster, ghost-desk, error, and idle states.

suggestions.title: Bloberto titles map is missing 'in-flow' vibe
suggestions.description: BLOBERTO_TITLES in Office.jsx maps crushing→CVO, on-fire→CFO, slow-day→CDO, in-flow→CPO, after-hours→CISO. Wait — in-flow IS there (CPO). Never mind. But the title map uses 'in-flow' as a key while the VIBE_VERSIONS and other maps use it differently. Just confirming consistency — all maps verified. No issue, just paranoia doing its job.

summary:
Review #59 shows meaningful progress: Carlos delivered cleanly on all five assigned fixes (titleIdxRef, footer counter, dead updatedAt prop, after-hours greeting, TASK_TAG_DEFS pattern). Maya resolved the plantToast/completionToast overlap with a vertical offset. The mobile invisibility disaster from #58 is gone — the office renders on mobile now. These are real wins.

However, the two escalated issues have now reached STRIKE 16 with no action. overflow:visible lets the office bleed into the page — confirmed visually in the desktop screenshot with the sunrise window escaping the container boundary. aria-live on conditional elements continues to ghost-announce to screen readers on every vibe change, every completion, every full-sync. These are not 'nice to have' fixes. One is a visual defect visible to everyone; the other actively degrades the experience for screen reader users.

New issues: a speech bubble covering the tab nav on mobile, duplicate CSS rules that nobody cleaned up, micro-font labels at 0.38-0.45rem that exist only as philosophical gestures toward text, and the inline toast styles remain untouched below the overlap fix.

Bloberto's Office is charming, feature-rich, and clearly built with care. It also has the same two critical bugs it had sixteen reviews ago. Pick a sprint. Fix the overflow. Fix the aria-live. In that order.
overall_verdict: PASS WITH ESCALATIONS — two critical carry-overs at strike 16 require immediate resolution next sprint
